repo.description: Vim-fork focused on extensibility and usability
repo.name: neovim

body:
## Summary

- Clarify E77 as a single-file-name limit without making it glob-specific.
- Update help text for explicit or glob-expanded multiple file names and point glob users to `:next`.
- Add a functional test for `:edit` with a glob argument.

Closes #12139

## Test

- `git diff --check`

comment_data.body: E77 is raised whenever a single-file command receives more than one file name, not exclusively from glob expansion. For example, `:edit file1 file2` (no wildcard) will also produce this error, and the new parenthetical "(glob expansion is not supported here)" will be misleading in that case. Consider wording that covers both cases, e.g. "E77: Too many file names (only one allowed here)", and keep the glob-specific guidance in the help text/hint rather than the error string. Additionally, modifying a long-standing Vim error message string can break user scripts, plugins, and translations that match on the exact text — worth confirming this trade-off is intentional.

The explanatory paragraph below still says \"When expanding file names, more than one match was found\", which only describes the glob case. Since E77 can also fire for explicitly listed multiple file names, consider broadening this description (e.g., \"More than one file name was given (either explicitly or via glob expansion). Only one is allowed for the command that was used.\") so the help is consistent with all cases that raise the error.
comment_data.diff_hunk:
@@ -548,11 +548,13 @@ into this limit, you have used too many |:highlight| commands with different
 arguments.  A ":highlight link" is not counted.
 
 							*E77*  >
-  Too many file names
+  Too many file names (glob expansion is not supported here)
 
 When expanding file names, more than one match was found.  Only one match is
 allowed for the command that was used.

comment_data.body: Deleting `glob_files` in both `before_each` and `after_each` for every test in this describe block adds per-test overhead for tests that don't use these files. Consider scoping the setup/teardown to a nested `describe` block around the new `:edit` glob test, or performing the create+delete inside the test itself, so unrelated tests aren't affected.
comment_data.diff_hunk:
@@ -22,8 +23,19 @@ describe('nlua_call_excmd excmds', function()
 end)
 
 describe('excmds', function()
+  local glob_files = { 'Xedit_glob_one.js', 'Xedit_glob_two.js' }
+
   before_each(function()
     clear()
+    for _, file in ipairs(glob_files) do
+      fn.delete(file)
+    end
+  end)
+
+  after_each(function()
+    for _, file in ipairs(glob_files) do
+      fn.delete(file)
+    end

comment_data.body:
why isn't this test in `test/functional/ex_cmds/edit_spec.lua` ?

this is the kind of thing [you are expected to check](https://github.com/neovim/neovim/blob/master/CONTRIBUTING.md#ai-assisted-work) before submitting an AI-assisted PR
comment_data.diff_hunk:
@@ -66,6 +78,17 @@ describe('excmds', function()
     command('command')
   end)
 
+  it(':edit with glob reports that glob is not allowed', function()

comment_data.body: Moved this into `test/functional/ex_cmds/edit_spec.lua` and kept the glob setup scoped with that test. Verified with `make functionaltest TEST_FILE=test/functional/ex_cmds/edit_spec.lua`; the current head is also green (35 success, 6 skipped).
comment_data.diff_hunk:
@@ -66,6 +78,17 @@ describe('excmds', function()
     command('command')
   end)
 
+  it(':edit with glob reports that glob is not allowed', function()

comment_data.body: these operations should be moved to the `it('with glob reports that glob is not allowed` test itself, we dont want to run them for other `it` tests
comment_data.diff_hunk:
@@ -4,12 +4,25 @@ local n = require('test.functional.testnvim')()
 local eq, command, fn = t.eq, n.command, n.fn
 local ok = t.ok
 local matches = t.matches
+local pcall_err = t.pcall_err
+local write_file = t.write_file
 local clear = n.clear
 local feed = n.feed
 
 describe(':edit', function()
+  local glob_files = { 'Xedit_glob_one.js', 'Xedit_glob_two.js' }
+
   before_each(function()
     clear()
+    for _, file in ipairs(glob_files) do
+      fn.delete(file)
+    end
+  end)
+
+  after_each(function()
+    for _, file in ipairs(glob_files) do
+      fn.delete(file)
+    end

comment_data.body: I find the ` (glob not allowed)` misleading here since as explained just below, it doesn't necessarily implies a glob was used
comment_data.diff_hunk:
@@ -548,10 +548,12 @@ into this limit, you have used too many |:highlight| commands with different
 arguments.  A ":highlight link" is not counted.
 
 							*E77*  >
-  Too many file names
+  Too many file names (glob not allowed)

comment_data.body: Moved the glob file create/delete work into the glob-specific test in 36e03ab13, so the other :edit tests only run clear().
comment_data.diff_hunk:
@@ -4,12 +4,25 @@ local n = require('test.functional.testnvim')()
 local eq, command, fn = t.eq, n.command, n.fn
 local ok = t.ok
 local matches = t.matches
+local pcall_err = t.pcall_err
+local write_file = t.write_file
 local clear = n.clear
 local feed = n.feed
 
 describe(':edit', function()
+  local glob_files = { 'Xedit_glob_one.js', 'Xedit_glob_two.js' }
+
   before_each(function()
     clear()
+    for _, file in ipairs(glob_files) do
+      fn.delete(file)
+    end
+  end)
+
+  after_each(function()
+    for _, file in ipairs(glob_files) do
+      fn.delete(file)
+    end

comment_data.body: Adjusted this in 36e03ab13: E77 now says "Too many file names (only one allowed)", and the doc text below keeps the glob-specific `:next` hint.
comment_data.diff_hunk:
@@ -548,10 +548,12 @@ into this limit, you have used too many |:highlight| commands with different
 arguments.  A ":highlight link" is not counted.
 
 							*E77*  >
-  Too many file names
+  Too many file names (glob not allowed)

comment_data.body:
we are going in circles. this was already settled in https://github.com/neovim/neovim/issues/12139

the minor risk of "misleading" is outweighed by the UX improvement for other cases
comment_data.diff_hunk:
@@ -548,10 +548,12 @@ into this limit, you have used too many |:highlight| commands with different
 arguments.  A ":highlight link" is not counted.
 
 							*E77*  >
-  Too many file names
+  Too many file names (glob not allowed)

comment_regular_data.body:
(see previous: #39829)

Please refrain from using copilot for future PRs; it's noisy. Just run copilot on your own machine.

I checked the current failed Windows functional run. The changed `ex_cmds` group, including `test/functional/ex_cmds/edit_spec.lua`, passed on Windows. The failure happened later in unrelated `plugin`, `treesitter`, `ui`, `vimscript`, and `terminal` groups with Nvim processes exiting unexpectedly (`0xc0000142` / EOF from Nvim).

I tried to rerun the failed job, but GitHub rejected the rerun with `Must have admin rights to Repository`. I do not see a branch-local patch indicated by this failure.

title: fix(ex_cmds): improve E77 glob expansion message
